Course Title: Digital Twins and AI: Accuracy vs Risk
Category: Manufacturing: Chemicals, Food Manufacturing, Industrial Manufacturing, Textiles, Automotive, Pharmaceuticals, Electronics
Who is this for: Manufacturing Engineers, Production Managers, Quality Assurance Managers, Plant Managers, Operations Supervisors, Process Engineers, Manufacturing Technicians, Production Planners, Industrial Engineers
Team or Dept this is for: Manufacturing, Operations, Quality Assurance, Engineering, Production, Process Engineering
Description: When simulated environments don’t match real-world conditions.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with manufacturing processes and a general understanding of what digital tools or software are used for in an industrial setting is all that is required to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key differences between digital twin simulations and real-world manufacturing conditions, and explain the risks that arise when these environments fail to align. You will also apply practical strategies to assess model accuracy and reduce the potential impact of simulation errors across a range of manufacturing sectors, including chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and automotive production.
